summaryText: Pastor Travis continued the 'True Love' series by focusing on the critical role of communication in relationships. He explained that the world teaches a counterfeit love based on lust and feelings, but true love is defined by God's word in 1 Corinthians 13. The number one cause of relational breakdown, including divorce, is not money or infidelity but poor communication, specifically unmet expectations that are never voiced and become 'silently weaponized.' The sermon centered on James 1:19: 'Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to become angry.' Pastor Travis unpacked this verse as the 'sound of love,' detailing three key principles. First, true love listens first, creating safety and showing others they matter. Second, true love speaks slowly, understanding the life-and-death power of words and choosing them with care and intention. Third, true love responds instead of reacts, recognizing that anger is often a symptom of deeper hurts and unmet needs. He encouraged practical application, including repentance for hurtful words, asking about a partner's needs, and committing to growth. The message concluded with a call to use words for healing and building up, both in earthly relationships and for eternal life through confessing Jesus as Lord.
mainPoints: True love listens first, creating safety and showing others they matter.
True love speaks slowly, understanding the life-and-death power of words.
True love responds instead of reacts, recognizing anger as a symptom of deeper hurts.

versesMentioned.text: Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love.

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.
To answer before listening— that is folly and shame.
Those who guard their mouths and their tongues keep themselves from calamity.
The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
Whoever is patient has great understanding, but one who is quick-tempered displays folly.
If you declare with your mouth, "Jesus is Lord," and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
being confident of this, that he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.

Monday.scripture: James 1:19 (NIV) - 'Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.'
Monday.action: In one conversation today, make a conscious effort to listen without interrupting or planning your response.
Monday.prayer: God, help me to be quick to listen today. Quiet my mind and heart so I can truly hear the person speaking to me.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 18:21 (NIV) - 'The tongue has the power of life and death...'
Tuesday.action: Before speaking in a potentially tense situation, pause and ask: 'Is what I'm about to say true, necessary, and loving?'
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, guard my mouth. Help me to use my words to bring life and build others up today.

Wednesday.scripture: Ephesians 4:29 (NIV) - '...only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs...'
Wednesday.action: Intentionally speak one encouraging, affirming word to your spouse, a family member, or a friend.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, shape my speech. Show me how to use my words to meet the needs of others and encourage them.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 14:29 (NIV) - 'Whoever is patient has great understanding...'
Thursday.action: When you feel frustration rising, take three deep breaths before you respond. Identify the deeper need or hurt behind your initial reaction.
Thursday.prayer: Father, when I feel anger, help me to be slow to become angry. Give me understanding and self-control.

Friday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-5 (NIV) - 'Love is patient, love is kind... it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs.'
Friday.action: If a past hurtful word comes to mind, consciously choose to release it and not hold it against the person. If led, offer or seek forgiveness.
Friday.prayer: God of covenant love, thank you for your forgiveness. Help me to extend that same grace and let go of records of wrongs.

Saturday.scripture: Philippians 1:6 (NIV) - '...he who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on one area of communication (listening, speaking, or managing anger) where you've made progress this week. Thank God for that growth.
Saturday.prayer: Thank you, Lord, for the good work you are doing in me. I praise you for the progress, however small, and trust you to complete your work in my relationships.
